mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points triggers recession risk premiums, causing immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in consumer credit expansion (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) confirms robust aggregate demand without supply bottlenecks, driving earnings revisions upward and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over a 3-year forward test, or the interaction term shows a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression of forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals capacity utilization collapse, which historically precedes equity drawdowns and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or neutral when industrial production breaks below its trend, indicating the signal captures noise rather than structural capacity decline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect forward-looking business and consumer confidence, which translates into immediate equity risk appetite and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal exhibits a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or the predictive R-squared drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, real borrowing costs become restrictive, compressing corporate profit margins and equity valuations, which generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat when the policy rate crosses 4.5 percent, or forward returns show no significant difference between high-rate and low-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and real GDP (GDPC1) confirms a self-reinforcing expansion cycle, driving institutional capital inflows and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns when both series expand, or the interaction effect vanishes after controlling for market beta and momentum factors.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in consumer credit outstanding (RSAFS) during a high-inflation environment signals purchasing power exhaustion, triggering immediate equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when credit contracts alongside elevated inflation, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.5 over a 5-year backtest.
